title: UW-Madison Google Workspace  - Uploading Files to Google Drive
body: Note : The content of this document is based on information available from Google's support documentation . For the most up-to-date information and additional details, please refer to the original source. Google Drive Before uploading files or folders to Google Drive, you should first verify your upload settings. Verify upload settings Uploading files or folders Verify upload settings Log in to the UW-Madison Google Workspace: UW-Madison Google Workspace - Log in From the Gear Menu in the upper right, select Settings Locate the Uploads setting. Check or uncheck the box that corresponds to Convert uploads to Google Docs editor format . Checked: Spreadsheets, documents, and images in supported formats uploaded to Google Drive will be converted to a format that is editable in Google Docs. Click here for more information. This setting is most appropriate if you plan to use Google Docs to create and edit files in addition to sharing them. Unchecked: Files uploaded to Google Drive will not be automatically converted to a format that is editable in Google Drive. You may always convert files manually at a later time. This setting is most appropriate if you plan to use Google Docs primarily to share documents with colleagues and not edit them with Google Docs. Once you have verified that your upload settings meet your needs, you are ready to start uploading files! Back to the Top Uploading files or folders Log in to the UW-Madison Google Workspace: UW-Madison Google Workspace - Log in Click the New button in the upper left corner Select File Upload or Folder Upload The Folder option is not available in Firefox or Safari. If you select Enable folder upload , you will be prompted to download Google Chrome. Some Microsoft Edge users may see a message that says " No upload methods available. Please check your system configuration" . If you see this message, you will need to install the Google Chrome Frame add-on before uploading files. You will then be prompted to select files or folders. Once your files or folders have uploaded, you will see a confirmation window notifying you that your upload is complete in the lower right corner: Back to the Top See Also UW-Madison Google Workspace  - Share Files and Folders in Google Drive UW-Madison Google Workspace  - Exporting Multiple Files from Google Drive UW-Madison Google Workspace -  UW-Madison Google Workspace Accounts vs. Personal Google Accounts
